mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates for future cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in > 40% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values indicate expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during early-cycle phases, with mean forward return <= 0.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 235.0, elevated inflation expectations trigger immediate equity sell-offs as investors price in tighter monetary policy and margin compression, yielding negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL values above 235.0 are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting consumer spending and corporate profitability, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative 60-day returns in > 45% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue generation, directly supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02 across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index exceeding 0.5 percent signals sudden supply-side contraction and weakening manufacturing demand, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO drops greater than 0.5 percent are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 55% of cases.
